qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary code execution via custom component eval/exec in Langflow API endpoints
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: No match: the finding describes custom-component eval/exec code execution through /api/v1/custom_component and related code paths, while the advisory describes an IDOR caused by missing Flow.user_id ownership scoping in _read_flow for GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/flow/{flow_id}.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. GHSA-8c4j-f57c-35cf is a Langflow flow-object authorization flaw in src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/flows.py where _read_flow failed to scope Flow queries to the requesting user when authentication was enabled. The only qualifying session finding concerns arbitrary Python execution via custom component eval/exec paths and does not describe the flow read/update/delete ownership check.
evidence: Advisory details identify IDOR in GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/flow/{flow_id} caused by _read_flow omitting Flow.user_id == user_id ownership filtering.
The qualifying finding lists /api/v1/custom_component, /api/v1/custom_component/update, /api/v1/validate/code, PythonCodeStructuredTool, and LambdaFilterComponent eval/exec paths.
The finding's code locations are endpoints.py, custom/eval.py, utils/validate.py, python_code_structured_tool.py, and lambda_filter.py, not flows.py or _read_flow.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so advisory-specific PoC execution was not applicable.
